pi_items.source: task.json instructions and export_result.sh explicitly demand this output path.
task.json explicitly instructs extracting data exactly 60 seconds BEFORE the origin time.
task.json explicitly instructs extracting data exactly 600 seconds AFTER the origin time.
verifier.py is missing from the task directory, so this programmatic tolerance limit is never enforced and cannot be verified.
Inspection of /examples/seiscomp_env/data/fdsn/ directory confirms there are exactly 3 GEOFON station .mseed files (GSI, SANI, BKB) bundled as available data.
verifier.py is missing from the task directory, so this programmatic duration upper bound is never enforced and cannot be verified.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real GEOFON/IRIS seismic data for the 2024 Noto Peninsula earthquake. The true origin time in the database is 2024-01-01T07:10:09.476Z. The bundled dataset contains exactly 3 GEOFON station records (GSI, SANI, BKB), validating the 3 min_stations claim. The required time window is strictly 60s before and 600s after the origin time, outputting to /home/ga/noto_event_data.mseed. The tolerance and duration bounds in metadata are unverified due to the omission of the verifier.py script.
